understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to get any pushable object (either the selected piece or any pushable block) to reach or exceed a target x-coordinate. The game tracked the position of controllable pieces and blocks, and winning required at least one pushable object to reach the target_x value specified in the level state.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement was grid-based with one STEP equaling exactly 3 pixels. The selected piece could move in four cardinal directions (left, right, up, down) via MOVE actions. If a pushable object (block) was adjacent in the direction of movement, it would be pushed along with the selected piece by one STEP. SELECT actions allowed switching between controllable objects. Walls and boundaries could block movement, but their exact blocking rules required testing—some might block movement differently for the selected piece versus objects being pushed. The semantic_grid provided a quick visual reference (P=selected, B=block, #=wall, .=empty), and movement hints were available but not definitive.
